title: Electrical, ESD, Battery, Fire, Lifting, and Workspace Safety · PBQ Checkpoint
summary: A support panel compares near-miss cases for electrical, esd, battery, fire, lifting, and workspace safety and accepts only safe, authorized, causal, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible decisions. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for safe work authorization and hazard-control plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Survey electrical, thermal, chemical, mechanical, trip, ergonomic, fire, and data hazards before selecting controls.
Match grounding, antistatic bags, mats, humidity, handling, and packaging controls to static-sensitive work.
Recognize energized, capacitor, liquid, damaged-battery, odor, heat, and deformation stop conditions and route them to qualified handling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Hazard Survey
steps.instruction:
Order the Hazard Survey CompTIA A+ support HTTP evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Hazard Survey.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Hazard Survey assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For CompTIA A+ support Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Hazard Survey, keep Hazard Survey connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A repair bench combines a swollen battery, energized supply, heavy printer, loose jewelry, blocked exit, poor ventilation, and no disposal authority. The quiz variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Survey electrical, thermal, chemical, mechanical, trip, ergonomic, fire, and data hazards before selecting controls.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Hazard Survey has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Environmental Disposal
steps.instruction:

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate reuse, data sanitization, battery handling, hazardous waste, recycling, and jurisdiction-specific documentation.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Hazard Survey to the changed stakeholder case.
